differences.title: 1. ROUTING
differences.leftDesc: Convention over configuration, deeply integrated with data loading.
differences.rightDesc: Flexible routing with both file-based and programmatic options.

differences.title: 2. DATA LOADING
differences.leftDesc: Colocated with routes, simpler data fetching and mutations.
differences.rightDesc: Separate data fetching functions, more configuration needed.

differences.title: 3. MUTATIONS
differences.leftDesc: Built-in, declarative client-side handling of server actions.
differences.rightDesc: Requires separate API routes or newer Server Actions.

differences.title: 4. RENDERING
differences.leftDesc: Primarily server-rendered, client-side hydration is key.
differences.rightDesc: Offers SSR, SSG, ISR, and client-side rendering.

differences.title: 5. ESTABLISHMENT
differences.leftDesc: Focuses on web standards, rapid iteration.

differences.title: 6. ARCHITECTURE
differences.leftDesc: Server handles most logic, minimal client-side JS.

differences.title: 7. PERFORMANCE
differences.leftDesc: Minimal JavaScript for faster initial load.
